design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate linearly through fortress, encounter one-on-one fights with enemies, manage health regeneration timing, reach and rescue the princess
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health attrition balanced against regeneration mechanic; one-on-one duels with escalating difficulty; environmental hazards and final boss with conditional victory state
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial artist ascending a mountain fortress to rescue a princess from a warlord, drawing on cinematic action film tropes and samurai narrative
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes cinematic storytelling and atmospheric world-building over mechanical complexity; treats fighting as a narrative device rather than the primary game mechanic; uses screen wipes and cutscenes inspired by Kurosawa films; final encounter requires non-fighting stance approach, subverting typical rescue mechanics with an unexpected puzzle element

difficulty.lives_system: Single life; game ends when all health is lost, requiring complete restart
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints; failure requires restart from beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Health regeneration mechanic rewards defensive play and spacing; player can control approach distance and attack timing to manage encounters; final boss requires learning correct stance behavior rather than combat skill

progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through fortress; each screen presents a new enemy encounter or environmental hazard; story advances through mandatory cutscenes showing Mariko and Akuma
replayability_hooks: Easter egg: floppy disk can be flipped upside-down to play vertically mirrored version
Final boss has conditional victory state requiring specific approach, encouraging second playthroughs to discover mechanic
Health regeneration mechanics create variable encounter duration based on player spacing strategy

world_building_techniques: Cutscenes showing antagonist Akuma ordering attacks and princess Mariko awaiting rescue
Screen wipes adapted from Kurosawa's Seven Samurai for cinematic pacing
Silent/minimal dialogue environmental storytelling, inspired by early Disney films and silent pictures
Linear level design framed as ascent up mountain into fortress stronghold
Sound design as narrative tool (father Francis Mechner composed soundtrack)
Visual art style inspired by Japanese Ukiyo-e woodblock prints and manga

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss (Akuma) designed as puzzle encounter wrapped in combat framing; victory requires learning that approaching in non-fighting stance is mandatory, subverting player expectations from prior encounters
notable_bosses: Akuma - final boss with conditional victory state; player must approach in neutral stance or receive instant death kick from princess if in fighting stance

faction_indicators: Akuma as tyrannical fortress warlord commanding subordinate fighters
Princess Mariko as captive within power structure
Ascending male protagonist as external force challenging established hierarchy
danger_signals: Akuma's trained hawk as extension of antagonist's control
Environmental hazards (cliffside, falling portcullis) as fortress defenses
Progressive difficulty of enemy encounters as ascending threat
Castle fortress setting as symbol of isolated, fortified power
environmental_storytelling: Mountain ascent framing journey as physical and narrative climb
Fortress architecture contains story progression (gates, cells, chambers)
Mariko's cell shown in cutscenes, establishing spatial narrative
Progression from exterior mountain to interior chambers to final confrontation
